title: manihot palmata var. genuina
intro: Manihot palmata var. genuina, also known as the wild cassava or manioc, is a type of plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and its edible roots and leaves have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. The roots and leaves of the plant can be prepared in various ways, including as a decoction, tea, or powder, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and respiratory problems.
